response.text:
## REFLECTION

Household r3c2 is in a precarious energy situation: with only 1.37 kWh of a 3 kWh capacity (45.7% SoC) and a 1.00 kW load, the battery will be depleted in approximately 1.4 hours if no external energy arrives. The memory log reveals a consistent pattern of incoming requests from geographic neighbors (r2c2, r3c3, r4c2) all reporting SoC fractions between 0.35–0.49, indicating widespread energy scarcity across the grid. Notably, r3c2 has recently received multiple ACCEPT messages from these same neighbors, suggesting they are willing to share—yet the current SoC has not improved, implying either delivery delays, network congestion, or that accepted offers have not yet materialized. With no established trust circles and operating under fallback geographic round-robin logic, r3c2 must adopt a conservative sharing posture to preserve its own survival margin while remaining open to reciprocal support from neighbors who have demonstrated willingness to help. The islanded state means no external grid connection is available.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.10
recipient_priority:
  - circle: geographic
    weight: 1.0
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Grid-wide scarcity detected; r3c2 SoC critically low at 45.7%. Neighbors r2c2, r3c3, r4c2 have accepted prior requests but energy not yet received—prioritize incoming offers. Conservative sharing to preserve minimum operating margin while maintaining reciprocal relationships."
ttl_ticks: 3
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
